description: Exact, committed reconstruction recipe for the truck-3k-run6 result reported in README.md and REPRODUCE.md. This manifest captures the fetch -> ingest -> train -> eval command chain so the run is reconstructable WITHOUT committing the large .aura checkpoint (which is git-ignored). Re-running these commands on a GPU machine reproduces run6.
status: recipe committed; the checkpoint itself is NOT committed (binary, git-ignored). Reproduce via the commands below.

scene.seeding: point-seeded (one carrier per COLMAP SfM point, 3DGS-style)

fetch.note: Tanks & Temples requires registration; see the script for the exact manual steps and expected layout. Datasets are git-ignored and never committed.

ingest.note: --point-seeded produces one carrier per SfM point (~129,531 for truck). Deterministic given the COLMAP model.

flags.optimizer_type: adam (set internally by the train subcommand)

seeds.policy: deterministic without an explicit seed flag
seeds.note: There is NO --seed flag on `aura.cli train`. Carrier seeding is deterministic from the COLMAP sparse model (one carrier per SfM point with --point-seeded); training iterates carriers/targets in a fixed order; evaluation uses deterministic ray ordering with no random sampling. The same manifest + flags reproduce the same run.

eval.metrics: PSNR, SSIM (11x11 Gaussian window), LPIPS (if `pip install lpips` available)
eval.note: The reported run6 numbers (see README.md / REPRODUCE.md) were measured on-GPU with the CUDA renderer at 0.125x scale. This config file does NOT restate the result numbers; it captures only the recipe. The eval fails loudly rather than mislabelling a CPU fallback as cuda.

baseline.purpose: Executed-vs-executed 3DGS comparison on the SAME scene + eval split.
baseline.note: Status: implemented; pending GPU run. Requires gsplat. After this writes outputs/eval_truck_baseline3dgs.txt, `python scripts/run_aura_vs_3dgs.py --eval-dir outputs` tabulates AURA vs the real baseline.
